steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence within the text as a whole. Read it in context: the first three sentences establish that visitor travel to Olympic National Park generates carbon emissions. The underlined sentence then says policymakers could reduce those emissions by encouraging people to visit closer parks. That is a proposed remedy, not a new problem or a restatement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It presents a harm that is unique to the location described earlier in the text.’: The underlined sentence does not introduce a harm — it proposes a solution. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It indicates that policymakers have been working on a certain problem for a long time.’: The sentence describes what policymakers “could” do, not what they have been doing. There is no indication of duration. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It suggests a course of action to address the problem described earlier in the text.’: The sentence recommends that policymakers encourage visits to closer parks — a direct course of action aimed at the carbon emission problem established in sentences 1–3. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘It rephrases the question raised in the first sentence of the text.’: The first sentence does not raise a question; it describes what visitors need. The underlined sentence adds new information rather than rephrasing anything. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
